hero_title: Skin screening that actually makes sense.
hero_desc: We built SkinShield because early detection shouldn't require a specialist appointment. Answer a few questions, upload a photo, and know what to do next.
built_for_title: Built for people who don't know where to start.
built_for_p1: Most people notice a skin issue and either ignore it or spiral into a Google search that leads nowhere good. SkinShield gives you a calm, structured way to understand what you're looking at and what to actually do next.
built_for_p2: It's not a diagnosis. It's a starting point — and sometimes that's all you need.
step01_desc: Duration, itching, sun exposure — takes about 2 minutes.
step02_title: Upload a photo of the area
step02_desc: Any decent smartphone photo works. No special equipment needed.
step03_desc: Risk level, possible conditions, and what to do next — in plain English.
honest_p1: SkinShield is a college project. We're not doctors, and this isn't a medical product. What we are is a team that spent a lot of time making something genuinely useful — a tool that helps you understand your skin better and know when it's time to see someone.
honest_p2: The AI model is trained on real dermatology data. The reports are generated thoughtfully. But please, if something looks serious — go see a doctor.
cta_desc: It takes under a minute. No account required to see how it works.
symptom_desc: Complete this form before uploading your skin image.
q_main_concern: What is the main concern bringing you in today?
q_duration: How long have you had this rash/spot/acne?
q_symptoms: Does the area itch, burn, or hurt?
q_prior_treatment: Have you tried any creams or treatments already?
q_allergies: Do you have any known allergies or skin sensitivities?
q_skincare: What is your current daily skincare routine?
q_medications: Are you taking any medications or supplements?
q_family: Has anyone in your family had skin conditions/cancer?
q_sun: What is your typical sun exposure?
q_physical: Have you noticed any changes in size, shape, or color?
server_failed: Server connection failed. Is the backend running?
upload_desc: Clear and focused photos give better screening quality.
symptom_missing: Symptom data missing. Please go back and complete the questionnaire.
login_required: You must be logged in to run a scan. Please log in first.
upload_failed: Upload failed. Is the backend running?
no_prediction: No prediction found. Complete questionnaire and upload steps first.
disclaimer: This is AI screening, NOT a medical diagnosis. Always consult a dermatologist.
report_error: Could not load report. Please retry later.
register_subtitle: Start your free skin screening today
network_error: Network error. Make sure the backend is running.
about_title: We're students who wanted to build something real.
about_intro: SkinShield started as a final year project idea — what if we could use AI to help people catch skin problems early, especially in places where seeing a dermatologist isn't easy or affordable?
about_p1: We've all been in that situation — something looks off on your skin, you don't know if it's serious, and booking a dermatologist takes weeks. So you either panic or ignore it. Neither is great.
about_p2: SkinShield is our attempt at a middle ground. A quick, structured way to get a sense of what you're dealing with, understand the risk level, and know whether you need to act now or just keep an eye on it.
about_p3: We trained a MobileNetV3 model on the DermNet dataset — 23 skin condition classes, real clinical images, not just textbook examples. Combined with a symptom questionnaire and an AI-generated report, it gives you something more useful than a Google search and less scary than WebMD.
limitation_desc: We want to be upfront — SkinShield is a screening tool, not a diagnostic one. The AI can be wrong. Confidence scores aren't certainty. If you're worried about something on your skin, please see a doctor. We built this to help people take that step sooner, not to replace it.
ml_desc: MobileNetV3 trained on DermNet data with test-time augmentation. Outputs top 3 predictions with confidence scores.
ai_desc: A LangChain agent powered by Groq LLaMA and Gemini that turns ML output + symptom answers into a readable patient report.
backend_desc: Express API handling auth, image uploads to Cloudinary, scan history in MongoDB, and PDF generation.
frontend_desc: A clean, mobile-friendly interface built with Tailwind CSS. Works on any smartphone browser.
contact_intro: We're students, so we actually read our emails. Whether you have feedback, found a bug, or just want to know more — feel free to reach out.
email_reply: We usually reply within a day or two.
faq1_a: Reasonably, yes — for a student project. Our model hits around 94% on test data, but real-world skin photos are messier than training data. We always show confidence scores so you know how sure the model is.
faq2_q: Can I use this instead of going to a doctor?
faq2_a: No, please don't. Use it to decide whether to go — not instead of going. If the result says high risk, take it seriously.
faq3_a: It's stored securely on Cloudinary and tied to your account. We don't use it for anything other than generating your report.
faq4_a: Yes, completely. It's a college project. We're not monetising this.
card_upload_desc: Upload a clear smartphone image of the affected skin area.
camera_note: Camera opens on supported mobile browsers. On desktop, this may open file picker.
camera_error: Unable to access camera. Allow camera permission, or use file upload.
footer_desc: Early screening support for skin health awareness and quick next-step guidance.
footer_disclaimer: SkinShield is an early screening platform and not a final diagnosis.
footer_tagline: Built for awareness, speed, and accessibility.
